Status: DONE

# Task 02: Enemy Behavior Pass

Goal:

Make enemies feel more distinct instead of only being palette and silhouette variants.

Prompt:

Work only in the browser version under `C:\development\Project Shadow Shift\Web`.

Differentiate the existing enemy types through behavior:

- goblin should feel quick and scrappy
- shadow walker should feel stealthy or glide-like
- ash demon should feel heavy and dangerous
- void watcher should feel eerie and unusual

Possible improvements:

- unique patrol speeds
- short lunges or pauses
- distinct contact patterns
- floating movement for watcher
- different health or hit reactions

Keep the game playable and simple. Prefer readable handcrafted logic over complex systems.

When complete:

- change the top line to `Status: DONE`
- add a short bullet list under `Completion Notes`

Completion Notes:
- Added handcrafted per-enemy behavior rhythms so each type now reads differently in play.
- Goblins now scurry in quick bursts, shadow walkers pause and glide, ash demons wind up into heavy charges, and void watchers float and blink-shift.
- Tuned contact timing and visual readability so the new behaviors stay simple but easier to read and fight.
